message:
feat(frankenpandas): expand prelude with cut/qcut + to_datetime variants (br-ertxq / fd90.144)

fd90.144: completes the prelude module-level function surface to
match README's documented Module-Level Functions table (lines
678-691). Six more functions pub-used at the top level but missing
from prelude:

  cut, qcut                          — binning helpers
  timedelta_total_seconds            — Series.dt.total_seconds()
  to_datetime_with_format             — Custom strftime parser
  to_datetime_with_options             — Full ToDatetimeOptions
  to_datetime_with_unit               — Unit-aware epoch parser

After this commit the prelude exposes the entire documented module-
level function surface — cut/qcut binning, all four to_datetime
variants, both timedelta helpers, plus to_numeric.

Continues the prelude-completeness pattern from fd90.121/125/126/127/
128/130/141/142/143.

Verified:
- cargo build -p frankenpandas — compiles
- cargo test -p frankenpandas --lib — all 6 tests pass

Pure re-export expansion. No behavior change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-epko","title":"pandas: categorical value_counts category-awareness parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:56:24.940376903Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T11:22:18.091743832Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T11:22:18.089913068Z","close_reason":"Completed by frankenpandas-9v09 in commit cf8fbda","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-epoj","title":"[MEDIUM] dtype: no PeriodDtype / PeriodIndex — finance + econometric workflows unsupported","description":"/reality-check-for-project gap (parent br-frankenpandas-jbfn).

**Gap.** pandas exposes `pd.Period`, `pd.PeriodIndex`, `pd.PeriodDtype('Q-DEC')` for regular-frequency calendar periods (quarters, months, years, business days). Distinct from Timestamp + frequency — a Period is a *span* (Q1 2024), not an instant.

FrankenPandas coverage:
- fp-index: Index has Int64/Utf8/Timedelta64/Null label variants. No Period.
- fp-types DType: Null/Bool/Int64/Float64/Utf8/Categorical/Timedelta64. No Period.
- grep for `Period|pd\.Period|period_range` across crates returns zero hits.

**Impact.** Financial and econometric workflows — quarterly earnings, monthly GDP, daily business-day timestamps — rely on Period semantics. Users cannot port `.asfreq('Q')`, `PeriodIndex`, `Period('2024Q1') + 1`, or resample-to-Period idioms. Today's workaround: Utf8-encoded strings, which defeat the type-safety + arithmetic pandas provides.

**Fix shape.**
1. `fp-types::DType::Period { freq: PeriodFreq }` + `PeriodFreq` enum (Annual, Quarterly, Monthly, Weekly, Daily, BDay, Hourly, ...).
2. `fp-index::IndexLabel::Period(i64, PeriodFreq)` variant. i64 = ordinal offset from epoch.
3. `pd.period_range(start, end, freq)` → `period_range()` module fn.
4. `.asfreq('Q')` on a Series/DataFrame with datetime index.
5. Arithmetic: `Period + i64` shifts by n periods; `Period - Period` returns i64 period-count.
6. DISCREPANCIES.md entry DISC-N until full coverage.

**Blast radius.** ~300 LOC in fp-types (PeriodFreq arithmetic + ordinal↔calendar conversion), ~150 LOC in fp-index (PeriodIndex label variant + display), ~200 LOC in fp-frame (period_range + asfreq). ~20 conformance fixtures.

**Severity.** MEDIUM — domain-specific (finance / econometrics). Not in the first-wave pandas surface but quoted in the README's "pandas API target" claim.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-24T23:33:52.539057614Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T00:46:09.305328001Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T00:46:09.302855520Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dtype","parity-gap","period","review"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-eqyf","title":"Add dataframe isnull/notnull NaN and empty-string conformance packets","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:27:23.531990182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:31:38.494177546Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T22:31:38.492418388Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-ertxq","title":"fd90.144: cut/qcut + to_datetime variants + timedelta_total_seconds not in prelude (last stragglers)","description":"fd90.74-143 README audit. Continuing prelude-completeness — six more module-level functions documented in README's Module-Level Functions table (lines 678-689) but missing from prelude:
  - cut (line 689)
  - qcut (line 690)
  - timedelta_total_seconds (line 691)
  - to_datetime_with_format (lines 580, 1454)
  - to_datetime_with_options
  - to_datetime_with_unit

All pub-used at the top-level frankenpandas crate (lib.rs:50-54). cut/qcut and timedelta_total_seconds are only referenced in the function table (no actual code calls), but to_datetime_with_format appears in README troubleshooting at line 1454. None of them are in the documented prelude.

Continues the prelude-completeness pattern from fd90.121/125/126/127/128/130/141/142/143.

Fix: add all 6 to the prelude, completing the documented module-level surface. Verify with cargo build + cargo test -p frankenpandas --lib.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:39:57.301453163Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:41:34.836509084Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:41:34.833814640Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-et93","title":"pandas: to_datetime unit='m'|'h' numeric-origin parity","description":"py signature:
pd.to_datetime([0, 1, 2], unit="m")
pd.to_datetime([0, 1, 2], unit="h")

Current fp-frame DatetimeUnit::parse in crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs only accepts d/s/ms/us/ns and raises CompatibilityRejected for minutes/hours. Add minute/hour unit support across numeric conversion and origin handling with focused parity tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T16:56:14.234701039Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:07:37.383421483Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:07:37.381074280Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-eu4u","title":"fd90.69: fp-conformance needs crate-level rustdoc summarizing harness architecture","description":"Continuation of fd90.61-68 doc audit. Add crate-level //! rustdoc to fp-conformance - the largest test-only / harness crate (141 public items). Surface includes pandas-vs-frankenpandas conformance harness machinery: HarnessConfig + HarnessReport + run_smoke entry, OracleMode (Live vs Fixture), packet fixtures (FixtureDataFrame / FixtureSeries / FixtureProvenance / PacketFixture etc.), case results (CaseResult / CaseStatus / DriftLevel / ComparisonCategory), RequirementLevel for MUST/SHOULD/MAY tagging. Doc explains the role (conformance harness for spec-derived testing matching the testing-conformance-harnesses skill), mode dispatch (live oracle vs golden fixtures), and packet fixture format. Pure docs. Verify cargo doc cleanly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:26:20.658934856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T06:30:35.932020802Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T06:30:35.928904634Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","fd90","review"]}
